sections.summary: Document title only: Standard for Whey Cheeses (CXS 284‑1971); not a substantive provision.

sections.section: Formerly CODEX STAN A-7-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999, 2006. Amended in 2010, 2018, 2022.
sections.summary: Gives the standard's history: adopted 1971, revised 1999 and 2006, and amended in 2010, 2018 and 2022.

Records a 2022 amendment (CAC45) replacing the previous non‑retail container labelling text with a requirement that labelling of non‑retail containers follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).

Recommends that whey cheeses be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57‑2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and that products comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21‑1997.

States that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206‑1999), specific labelling provisions for whey cheeses apply.

Requires the food be named 'whey cheese', allows a descriptive term where needed for consumer information, permits omission of 'whey cheese' when an individual variety name is reserved by Codex or national legislation provided omission is not misleading, and allows designation or descriptive terms for cheeses obtained by whey concentration including fat‑based designations for unripened products.

Requires milk fat content to be declared to the final consumer in an acceptable manner (percentage by mass, percentage of fat in dry matter, or grams per serving with number of servings stated), and for cheeses from concentrated whey allows combined declaration with fat‑on‑dry‑basis categories: Creamed whey cheese minimum 33% fat on dry basis, Whey cheese minimum 10% and less than 33%, Skimmed whey cheese less than 10%.
Specifies that labelling of non‑retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).
States that methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234‑1999) relevant to this standard shall be used to check compliance.
